The board of directors of EDF has decided that the combined Shareholders' Meeting of the company, scheduled on 6 May 2021, will finally be held without the physical attendance of its shareholders. The decision follows, in particular, the Decree No. 2021-255 of 9 March 2021, which extended until 31 July 2021 the application period of the Ordinance No. 2020-321 of 25 March 2020, as amended, adapting the rules for meetings and deliberations of collective corporate bodies of legal persons and entities that are not legal entities under private law, due to the Covid 19 epidemic. The board noted that the process of selecting an independent director to be proposed to the shareholders to replace Laurence Parisot was still ongoing. The administrative measures limiting or prohibiting travel or collective gatherings, the closure of conference and meeting rooms and the obligation to comply with physical distancing measures, with regard to the number of persons usually present at previous Shareholders’ Meetings, prevent the physical presence of shareholders at the meeting. The meeting will be broadcast live on the company's website, and the video will also be available on replay within the period provided for by the regulations. The procedures for voting by post will be described in the convening notice which will be published by EDF.

K92 Mining 2022 operational guidance forecasts a significant production increase, with high margin production forecasted in 2022, with cash costs between $560 to640 per ounce gold and all in sustaining costs of between $890 to970 per ounce of gold. CHiQ, a high end household appliance brand, was named one of the top 10 consumer electronics brands for 2021 and its product, the Q8R MaX, took home the gold innovation award for artificial intelligence and display technology.

The Sortis Income Fund, LLC generated a net annualized return of 9.5% during the fourth quarter of 2020. ABN AMRO reported a modest loss of EUR 45 million for FY2020. The financial results for 2020 were marked by the impact of Covid 19 and large exceptional client files. The resulting ROE for 2020 was an unsatisfactory 0.8%, with a fully loaded Basel III CET1 ratio of 17.7% and the cost/income ratio was 66%. ABN is making good progress in the wind down of CIB non core as we have reduced the portfolio by around 45% since the second quarter. The Dutch economy and housing market remain resilient, with FY impairments below guidance and expected to decline in 2021. ABn AMRO has once again achieved a high score in the S&P Global’s SAM Corporate Sustainability Assessment, putting it in the top 10% of most sustainable banks worldwide. The company remains committed to resuming payments of its dividend sustainably.

NorthWest Value Partners Inc. announced today that it has acquired 395,257 trust units of NorthWest Healthcare Properties Real Estate Investment Trust pursuant to a private placement first announced by the REIT in February 2021. MuniFin has applied for the notes to be admitted to trading on the Helsinki Stock Exchange maintained by Nasdaq Helsinki. The company is owned by Finnish municipalities, the public sector pension fund Keva and the Republic of Finland. Lending is used for environmentally and socially responsible investment targets such as public transportation, sustainable buildings, hospitals and healthcare centres, schools and day care centres, and homes for people with special needs. The notes bear interest at a fixed rate of 0.26% per annum. The offering circular and supplemental offering circular are available in English on the company’s website at www.munifin.fi. The public trading is expected to commence on 12 April 2021.

Aktia Bank reported a strong start to the year and good income development lifted the comparable operating profit. The housing market was still active and demand for holiday homes continued to grow. Aktia’s strong competence within asset management was rewarded during the quarter when the independent fund rating agency Morningstar selected the best asset management houses in Finland in three categories: equity funds, fixed income funds and best overall. It feels really good to once again receive recognition for our long term work," said Mikko Ayub, CEO. The company's acquisition of the wealth management operations of Taaleri was completed on 30 April 2021. The comparable operating profits for 2021 is expected to be clearly higher than during 2020 provided that the circumstances on the market and in society are stable. The share of digital housing transactions increased further, with a strong balance growth of corporate credits.
